competitors.label: Array.prototype.sort()
competitors.selectionReason: MDN is the canonical baseline for Array.sort default behavior, mutation, and comparator rules.
theirStrengths: Array.prototype.sort() is a direct, credible comparator for this topic.
theirGaps: Array.prototype.sort() is less compressed around the specific interview framing and likely follow-up confusion this page targets.

realWorldUseCases: Production pitfall Array.prototype.sort() looks harmless but it mutates the original array and sorts values as strings by default. That is why numeric lists, shared state, and custom ordering logic fail in ways that are frustrating to debug.
followUpConfusion: Production pitfall Array.prototype.sort() looks harmless but it mutates the original array and sorts values as strings by default. That is why numeric lists, shared state, and custom ordering logic fail in ways that are frustrating to debug.
